AN APARTMENT COMPLEX ON PRINCETON AVENUE SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE.
TREES DOWN ON ROMULUS ROAD NEAR SIPSY HIGH SCHOOL.

AND EF1 TORNADO ENTERED ALABAMA FROM MISSISSIPPI NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PLEASANT HILLS AND STATE LINE ROADS. TORNADO WAS AT ITS WIDEST POINT HERE OF 300 YARDS WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 90 MPH. SEVERAL SOFT AND HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AT THIS LOCATION. TORNADO CONTINUED NE CROSSING SHAKE RAG ROAD WHERE 2 BUILDINGS SUSTAINED MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US HWY 278 AND GLASS ROAD.

AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN NORTHWEST OF FAYETTE AND TRACKED EAST NORTHEAST DOWNING HUNDREDS OF TREES AND CAUSING MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL BUILDINGS. PATH LENGTH 6.5 MILES. MAX PATH WIDTH 500 YDS.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON HWY 18 NORTHWEST OF FAYETTE
WINDS OF 75 MPH DOWNED 50 PINE TREES IN A SWATH OF WIND DAMAGE 0.6 MI LONG AND 200 YDS WIDE SOUTH OF NAUVOO.

AN EF0 TORNADO SPUN UP AFTER THE NAUVOO MICROBURST. TRAVELING ENE, THE TORNADO CROSSED SLICK LIZARD ROAD. HERE A MOBILE HOME SUSTAINED MODERATE DAMAGE FROM A FALLEN TREE. TORNADO TRAVELED FURTHER ENE AND DESTROYED AN OUTBUILDING AND CAUSED SHINGLE DAMAGE ON A HOME. THE TORNADO LIFTED AFTER CROSSING AL HWY 5. ALONG THE PATH, DOZENS OF HARD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED.

AN EF1 TORNADO SPUN UP AFTER THE NAUVOO MICROBURST. TRAVELING ENE, THE TORNADO CROSSED SLICK LIZARD ROAD. HERE A MOBILE HOME SUSTAINED MODERATE DAMAGE FROM A FALLEN TREE. TORNADO TRAVELED FURTHER ENE AND DESTROYED AN OUTBUILDING AND CAUSED SHINGLE DAMAGE ON A HOME. THE TORNADO LIFTED AFTER CROSSING AL HWY 5. ALONG THE PATH, DOZENS OF HARD AND SOFTWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED.

THE EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 8 AND COUNTY ROAD 3074. THE TORNADO MOVED OVER SMITH LAKE AND DAMAGED THE ROOFS OF SIX HOMES NEAR COVE ROAD SOUTH. ONE HOME HAD SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE. THOUSANDS OF TREES WERE ALSO DOWNED CAUSING DAMAGE TO AT LEAST A DOZEN MORE RESIDENCES. THE TORNDAO DISSIPATED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 41 AND COUNTY ROAD 52 AT 6 AM CST. MAX ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 105 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 10.26 MILES. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 1000 YARDS.
WINDS OF 100 MPH KNOCKED DOWN HUNDREDS OF TREES SOUTH OF HOUSTON IN A SWATH OF DAMAGE 1.2 MILES LONG AND 1000 YDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. A DOZEN HOMES WERE DAMAGED DUE TO FALLING TREES.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTH OF ADDISON ALONG CR 41 NORTH OF HUNTER ROAD AND TRACKED ENE TO THE CULLMAN COUNTY LINE. THE TORNADO DAMAGED SEVERAL PLANES AND DESTROYED A HANGAR AT THE ADDISON MUNICIPAL AIRPORT. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. PATH LENGTH IN WINSTON COUNTY WAS 4.2 MILES.

CAMPBELL RD JUST OFF HWY 21 IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO WATER COVERING THE ROAD. UTILITY/FIRE CREWS ARE UNABLE TO REACH DOWNED TREE DUE TO DEPTH OF WATER. PORTIONS OF GRANTTOWN RD ARE ALSO COVERED WITH WATER.

ALL LANES OF HIGHWAY 21 SOUTH OF OXFORD ARE IMPASSIBLE DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. A STRANDED MOTORIST ON HIGHWAY 21 HAD TO BE RESCUED BY A SWIFT WATER RESCUE TEAM DUE TO THE HIGH WATER. MULIPLE OTHER ROADS IN AND SOUTH OF OXFORD WERE ALSO IMPASSIBLE DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.

HEAVY RAINS OVER 3 HOURS RESULTED IN A FLASH FLOOD OF VILLAGE CREEK AT AVENUE W IN ENSLEY. THE CREEK EXCEEDED FLOOD STAGE AT 930 PM AND REMAIN AT OR ABOVE FLOOD STAGE UNTIL 1115 PM. THE CREEK CRESTED AT 10.35 FEET AT ITS HIGHEST. IN 3 HOURS VILLAGE CREEK WENT UP 7 FEET.
